preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.19.3: HOTFIX — Meta manual-token modal: pasting an access token via right-click → 'Use my API key' on a brand-scoped tile (e.g. POG) silently failed with 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again', even though the token was correctly persisted to the brand vault. Root cause: ipcMain.handle('discover-meta-ids') in app/main.js spawned the Go binary with --config <global-config> only — the binary's loadConfig does NOT auto-merge .merlin-config-<brand>.json from disk, so the @@VAULT:metaAccessToken@@ placeholder (which the renderer wrote to the brand config file) never made it into the binary's Config struct, applyBrandVault had nothing to resolve, and cfg.MetaAccessToken stayed empty. Dashboard refresh works because it builds a strict brand-merged config in a temp file via buildStrictBrandConfig and passes THAT as --config (line ~6253). discoverMetaIds now mirrors the dashboard pattern exactly — temp config, brand-merged, deleted on completion (no decrypted credentials left on disk). REGRESSION GUARD comment added at the fix site documenting the live incident (brand=pog, 2026-04-28, screenshot in chat). v1.19.2: TWO landed-together features — (A) window-level paste/drop into the chat composer (replaces the v1.19.1 bulk-upload tile), and (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap from BFF (worker secret = single source of truth). | (A) Bulk-upload UX refactor — kill the tile, embrace window-level paste/drop. The dedicated 'Bulk upload' Magic-panel tile (shipped in v1.19.1) is retired. Modern apps (Linear, Slack, ChatGPT desktop, Claude.ai, Cursor) all handle file input as window-level paste/drop into the composer; the LLM sees attachments alongside the user's typed context and decides routing — much smarter than a rigid filename Jaro-Winkler matcher running on every drop. The IPC backend stays shipped, refactored into a reusable helper that's exposed to the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool — the LLM calls it on demand when batch routing makes sense. | (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap — every provider's clientID now flows from the BFF (Cloudflare Worker secret <FOO>_CLIENT_ID) through the exchange response into cfg.OAuthFoo.ClientID. Hardcoded values inside oauth.go's getFooOAuth factories become first-launch fallbacks only; the canonical value lives in one place — the Cloudflare secret. Closes the LinkedIn debug-loop class of incidents. New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 17 documents the four required call sites for adding a new OAuth provider.
whatsNew: Fixed: pasting a Meta access token via the right-click → 'Use my API key' modal on a brand-scoped tile now works end-to-end. Previously the discover step failed with 'metaAccessToken required' even though the token was correctly saved — the binary spawn passed only the global config, missing the brand's vault placeholder. Now uses the same brand-merged temp config the dashboard uses.

Paste screenshots or drag-drop files anywhere in the window — they attach to your next message as chips above the composer and Merlin decides what to do with them (Read the image, file it with a product, embed in an ad brief, whatever the context calls for).

Removed the dedicated Bulk upload tile from the Magic panel — the LLM is smarter about routing than a rigid filename matcher. The Go Jaro-Winkler matcher stays shipped, exposed as the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool that Merlin calls on demand when batch-filing 5+ files into product folders is clearly the right move.

Hardened (paste/drop chip flow): pasted clipboard images materialize to assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/ with a SHA-prefixed filename that contains zero user-controlled bytes (eliminates injection); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist enforced server-side; counter-based dragenter/dragleave prevents the flicker that single-boolean overlays produce when the cursor crosses child elements.

Hardened: every OAuth provider's client_id now flows from the Cloudflare Worker secret as the single source of truth. The binary's hardcoded values are first-launch fallbacks only — the canonical value lives in one place, never two. Closes a class of debug loops where 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the secret?' couldn't be answered (Wrangler doesn't reveal secret values).

Hardened: 35 new Go tests (oauth_bootstrap_test.go) source-grep both the binary and worker.js to fail CI on any drift between the hardcoded fallbacks. Add a new OAuth provider in 4 places? CI checks all 4. Miss one? Test fails before merge.
